summary: Create a new 'Product Display Page' to showcase products, including search and filter functionalities, and integrate it into the website's routing.

steps.description: Create a new React component file for the Product Display Page. This page will be responsible for fetching (or mocking) product data and rendering it using shadcn/ui components.
steps.details: Create `ProductDisplayPage.tsx` in the `client/src/pages/` directory. This component will include a header, search input, category filter, and a grid layout for product cards. Each product card will display an image, name, description, price, and a 'View Details' button. Mock product data will be used for initial development.

steps.description: Implement the UI for the Product Display Page using available shadcn/ui components.
steps.details:
Inside `client/src/pages/ProductDisplayPage.tsx`:
- Use `Input` for the search bar.
- Use `Select` for category filtering.
- Use `Card` (including `CardHeader`, `CardContent`, `CardFooter`, `CardTitle`, `CardDescription`) to display individual product information.
- Use `Button` for actions like 'View Details'.
- Utilize `lucide-react` for icons (e.g., `Search`, `Filter`).
- Implement basic state management with `useState` for search term and selected category to filter the mock product list dynamically.

steps.description: Integrate the new Product Display Page into the application's routing system.
steps.details: Modify `client/src/App.tsx` to add a new route. Import `ProductDisplayPage` and define a new `Route` with the path `/products` that renders the `ProductDisplayPage` component. This will make the page accessible via the URL.

steps.description: Add a navigation link to the new Product Display Page in the main application navigation (e.g., header or sidebar).
steps.details: Within `client/src/App.tsx` (or the relevant navigation component if separate), add a `<Link to="/products">Products</Link>` element to allow users to easily navigate to the new page from the main interface.

steps.description: Test the new Product Display Page to ensure it renders correctly and routing works as expected.
steps.details: Navigate to `/products` in the browser. Verify that the page loads, products are displayed in cards, and the search and filter functionalities (UI-wise) are present. Test searching for product names/descriptions and filtering by category.

risks: Potential styling conflicts with existing global CSS if not carefully managed (e.g., TailwindCSS classes).
The current implementation uses mock data; integrating with a real API will require additional steps for data fetching, loading states, and error handling.
If `react-router-dom` is not already installed or configured, additional setup will be required for routing.
rollback_strategy: Delete the `client/src/pages/ProductDisplayPage.tsx` file and revert the changes made to `client/src/App.tsx` to remove the new route and navigation link.
success_criteria: The website successfully builds and runs without errors. Navigating to `/products` displays the new Product Display Page with a list of product cards. The search input and category filter UI elements are visible and functional (filtering the mock data). A 'Products' link is present in the main navigation and correctly directs to the new page.
original_request: can you create a page that for displaying the products.
